title: Fix admin dashboard route registration and accessibility

requirements.summary: Verify and fix the /admin route registration in App.tsx TanStack Router configuration to ensure the route is properly defined with correct path '/admin' and component reference to AdminDashboardPage
acceptanceCriteria: The /admin route is registered in the TanStack Router routes array in App.tsx
The route component correctly references AdminDashboardPage from frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboardPage.tsx
The route definition follows the same pattern as other routes in the application (dashboard, profile, etc.)

file_operations.description: Verify that AdminDashboardPage is imported at the top of the file with the correct path 'frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboardPage.tsx'. Ensure the /admin route is registered in the TanStack Router routes array with path='/admin' and element={<AdminDashboardPage />}, following the same pattern as existing routes like dashboard, profile, and groups. If the route is missing or incorrectly configured, add or fix it in the routes array within the router configuration.

requirements.summary: Ensure the AdminDashboardPage component import statement in App.tsx uses the correct file path and module name from frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboardPage.tsx
acceptanceCriteria: AdminDashboardPage is imported at the top of App.tsx
Import uses correct casing and file extension (.tsx)
No import errors or module resolution issues

file_operations.description: Verify the import statement for AdminDashboardPage at the top of App.tsx. Ensure it reads 'import AdminDashboardPage from './pages/AdminDashboardPage';' with correct relative path, casing matching the actual filename, and no TypeScript/ESLint errors. If the import is missing, add it alongside other page imports. If the import path is incorrect, fix it to match the actual file location.

requirements.summary: Test that navigating to /admin URL renders the AdminDashboardPage component for authenticated admin users without showing 404 not found error
acceptanceCriteria: Visiting /admin in the browser loads the AdminDashboardPage
No 404 or route not found errors appear
Admin users with isAdmin=true can access the page
Non-admin users see appropriate authorization message

file_operations.description: Ensure the /admin route is properly wired into the TanStack Router configuration so that navigating to /admin in the browser renders the AdminDashboardPage component. Verify that the route definition includes the correct path and element props, and that it is included in the routes array passed to the router. The AdminDashboardPage itself already contains authorization logic to restrict access to admin users, so no additional guards are needed in the route definition.

requirements.summary: Verify the Admin navigation link in Layout.tsx correctly routes to /admin when clicked and is visible only to users with admin privileges
acceptanceCriteria: Admin link appears in Layout navigation menu for admin users
Clicking the Admin link navigates to /admin route
Link is hidden for non-admin users
Link uses proper TanStack Router Link component for navigation

file_operations.description: Verify that the Admin navigation link is present in the Layout navigation menu and conditionally rendered based on userProfile?.isAdmin. Ensure the link uses the TanStack Router Link component with to='/admin' prop. If the link is missing, add it to the navigation menu alongside other links (Dashboard, Browse, etc.) with conditional rendering: {userProfile?.isAdmin && <Link to='/admin'>Admin</Link>}. Ensure the link is visible only when the user profile has isAdmin set to true.
